grammar_topics.explanation_en: Used for real conditions in the present or future and their results in the present or future. Form: if + present tense, will + bare infinitive / imperative.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Dùng cho các điều kiện có thật ở hiện tại hoặc tương lai và kết quả của chúng. Cấu trúc: if + thì hiện tại, will + động từ nguyên mẫu / câu mệnh lệnh.
rules: Other modals (can, could, may, might, shall, should, have to, ought to) and 'be going to' can be used instead of 'will' in the result clause.

examples.sentence: If we continue to pollute our planet, future generations will suffer.
examples.translation: Nếu chúng ta tiếp tục làm ô nhiễm hành tinh, các thế hệ tương lai sẽ phải gánh chịu hậu quả.

examples.sentence: If the people from Greenpeace call, tell them I'll call them back later.
examples.translation: Nếu người từ Greenpeace gọi, hãy bảo họ tôi sẽ gọi lại sau.

grammar_topics.explanation_en: Used for hypothetical conditions in the present or future and their hypothetical results in the present or future. Form: if + past simple, would + bare infinitive.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Dùng cho các điều kiện giả định (không có thật) ở hiện tại hoặc tương lai và kết quả giả định của chúng. Cấu trúc: if + thì quá khứ đơn, would + động từ nguyên mẫu.
rules: Other modals (might, could) can be used instead of 'would' in the result clause.
In formal English, 'were' is often used instead of 'was' after 'if' for all persons.
The phrase 'If I were you' is standard for giving advice.

examples.sentence: What would the local government do if there was an earthquake in the area?
examples.translation: Chính quyền địa phương sẽ làm gì nếu có một trận động đất trong khu vực?

examples.sentence: If I were a gambler, I'd put money on Jim being late.
examples.translation: Nếu tôi là một người thích cá cược, tôi sẽ đặt tiền vào việc Jim sẽ đến muộn.

grammar_topics.explanation_en: Used for hypothetical conditions in the past and their results in the past. Form: if + past perfect, would + have + past participle.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Dùng cho các điều kiện giả định không có thật trong quá khứ và kết quả của chúng trong quá khứ. Cấu trúc: if + thì quá khứ hoàn thành, would + have + quá khứ phân từ (V3).
rules: Other modals (might, could, should) can be used instead of 'would' in the result clause.

examples.sentence: A lot more people would have been trapped by the flood if there hadn't been a warning.
examples.translation: Sẽ có nhiều người hơn bị mắc kẹt bởi trận lũ nếu không có lời cảnh báo.

grammar_topics.explanation_en: Used to connect a hypothetical past condition with a present result, or a hypothetical present condition with a past result.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Dùng để kết nối một điều kiện giả định trong quá khứ với kết quả ở hiện tại, hoặc một điều kiện giả định ở hiện tại với kết quả trong quá khứ.
rules: Past condition -> Present result: If + past perfect, would + bare infinitive.
Present condition -> Past result: If + past simple, would + have + past participle.

examples.sentence: You might not be in so much trouble if you hadn't started gambling.
examples.translation: Bạn có lẽ đã không gặp nhiều rắc rối như vậy nếu bạn không bắt đầu đánh bạc (quá khứ không bắt đầu -> hiện tại không rắc rối).

examples.sentence: If I were you, I would have made Paula buy her own lottery ticket.
examples.translation: Nếu tôi là bạn, tôi đã bắt Paula tự mua vé số cho cô ấy rồi (hiện tại là tôi -> quá khứ đã làm).

grammar_topics.explanation_en: More formal forms of conditionals where 'if' is omitted and the verb is inverted.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Các hình thức trang trọng hơn của câu điều kiện, trong đó 'if' bị lược bỏ và động từ được đảo lên trước chủ ngữ.
rules: First conditional: Should + subject + bare infinitive...
Third conditional: Had + subject + past participle...
Negative inversion: Put 'not' after the subject (e.g., Had we not...).

examples.sentence: Should the drought continue, many people will be forced to leave their villages.
examples.translation: Nếu đợt hạn hán này tiếp tục, nhiều người sẽ buộc phải rời bỏ làng mạc của mình.

examples.sentence: Had Charles Darwin not visited the Galapagos Islands, he might never have developed his theory of evolution.
examples.translation: Nếu Charles Darwin không đến thăm quần đảo Galapagos, ông ấy có lẽ đã không bao giờ phát triển được thuyết tiến hóa của mình.

grammar_topics.explanation_en: Various phrases and words that express conditional meanings.
grammar_topics.explanation_vi: Các cụm từ và từ ngữ khác nhau dùng để diễn đạt ý nghĩa điều kiện.
rules: As/so long as, Provided/Providing (that), On condition (that): 'miễn là', 'với điều kiện là'.
If it wasn't/weren't for, Were it not for, But for, If it hadn't been for, Had it not been for: 'nếu không vì/nếu không nhờ'.

examples.sentence: Unless governments act now, the environment is really going to suffer.
examples.translation: Trừ khi các chính phủ hành động ngay bây giờ, nếu không môi trường sẽ thực sự phải gánh chịu hậu quả.

examples.sentence: But for your help, I wouldn't have been able to quit gambling.
examples.translation: Nếu không có sự giúp đỡ của bạn, tôi đã không thể bỏ cờ bạc được.

items.sentence: If you stay with us for three days, ___
options: we'll have enough time to see a few sights.
give us a call and we'll come to pick you up.
you wouldn't get hiccups, would you?
more areas of natural beauty will be destroyed.
items.answer: we'll have enough time to see a few sights.
items.explanation: Condition: staying for 3 days. Result: having time to see sights. This is a first conditional (real possibility).

items.sentence: If you decide not to vote in the election, ___
options: they have to go through a complicated procedure.
you can't really complain about the government.
very few people would obey the law.
we might get served a bit more quickly.
items.answer: you can't really complain about the government.
items.explanation: The logical consequence of not voting is losing the moral right to complain about the government.

items.sentence: If you didn't eat your lunch so fast, ___
options: you wouldn't get hiccups, would you?
very few people would obey the law.
we'd be happy to give you a good reference.
I'm pretty sure they won't let you check in.
items.answer: you wouldn't get hiccups, would you?
items.explanation: Second conditional: hypothetical present. Eating fast causes hiccups.

items.sentence: If there were a few more people working, ___
options: we might get served a bit more quickly.
very few people would obey the law.
more areas of natural beauty will be destroyed.
they have to go through a complicated procedure.
items.answer: we might get served a bit more quickly.
items.explanation: Second conditional: hypothetical present. More staff would lead to faster service.

items.sentence: If you forget to take your passport, ___
options: I'm pretty sure they won't let you check in.
they have to go through a complicated procedure.
give us a call and we'll come to pick you up.
very few people would obey the law.
items.answer: I'm pretty sure they won't let you check in.
items.explanation: First conditional: a real potential problem at check-in.

items.sentence: If the hire car isn't there when you arrive, ___
options: give us a call and we'll come to pick you up.
you can't really complain about the government.
more areas of natural beauty will be destroyed.
they have to go through a complicated procedure.
items.answer: give us a call and we'll come to pick you up.
items.explanation: First conditional / Imperative result: instructions for a specific situation.

items.sentence: If the city continues to expand, ___
options: more areas of natural beauty will be destroyed.
very few people would obey the law.
we'd be happy to give you a good reference.
they have to go through a complicated procedure.
items.answer: more areas of natural beauty will be destroyed.
items.explanation: First conditional: a future result of a current trend.

items.sentence: If the police didn't fine people, ___
options: very few people would obey the law.
you wouldn't get hiccups, would you?
they have to go through a complicated procedure.
we might get served a bit more quickly.
items.answer: very few people would obey the law.
items.explanation: Second conditional: hypothetical consequence of no fines.

items.sentence: If you did decide to leave the company, ___
options: we'd be happy to give you a good reference.
they have to go through a complicated procedure.
very few people would obey the law.
I'm pretty sure they won't let you check in.
items.answer: we'd be happy to give you a good reference.
items.explanation: Second conditional (more formal with 'did decide'): hypothetical future result.

items.sentence: If MPs wish to resign, ___
options: they have to go through a complicated procedure.
you can't really complain about the government.
we'll have enough time to see a few sights.
we might get served a bit more quickly.
items.answer: they have to go through a complicated procedure.
items.explanation: Zero/First conditional: expressing a general rule or fact about the resignation process.

exercises.label: B
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using an appropriate conditional form, starting with the words given.

items.sentence: Sue didn't get me a birthday present, which is why I didn't get her one. -> If Sue ___.
items.answer: had got me a birthday present, I would have got her one
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past condition and result.

items.sentence: We didn't pay them on time because they made a mistake with our order. -> If they ___.
items.answer: hadn't made a mistake with our order, we would have paid them on time
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past.

items.sentence: The climate is undergoing such radical changes that scientists are worried. -> If ___.
items.answer: the climate wasn't/weren't undergoing such radical changes, scientists wouldn't be worried
items.explanation: Second conditional: hypothetical present situation.

items.sentence: Did California become an important area because they found gold there? -> Would ___?
items.answer: California have become an important area if they hadn't found gold there
items.explanation: Third conditional question: hypothetical past.

items.sentence: It's the sunset that attracts tourists to that place. -> If it ___.
items.answer: wasn't/weren't for the sunset, tourists wouldn't be attracted to that place
items.explanation: Second conditional: 'If it weren't for' + noun.

items.sentence: There was a traffic jam on the motorway so I was late for my interview. -> If there ___.
items.answer: hadn't been a traffic jam on the motorway, I wouldn't have been late for my interview
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past.

items.sentence: I suggest you use a sunblock, and then you won't go red. -> If I were ___.
items.answer: you, I would use a sunblock (and then you wouldn't go red)
items.explanation: Second conditional: 'If I were you' for advice.

items.sentence: The accident happened as a result of the driver's not paying attention to the road. -> If the driver ___.
items.answer: had been paying attention to the road, the accident wouldn't have happened
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past.

exercises.label: C
exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in bold. Add any other words you need.

items.sentence: If I (1) ___ (do) that then, I never (2) ___ (have) so many fantastic experiences.
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past condition.

items.sentence: If I (1) hadn't done that then, I never (2) ___ (have) so many fantastic experiences.
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past result.

items.sentence: If you (3) ___ (ask) me now, I would say that I knew nothing when I started, but I've learnt a huge amount.
items.explanation: Second conditional: hypothetical present/future.

items.sentence: In a typical day, I'll deal with complaints, check on the running of the park and lead guided tours, if any (4) ___ (book).
items.explanation: First conditional: real present/future possibility. Passive voice used.

items.sentence: If it's low season, I (5) ___ (have) time to check on forest management and fire control measures.
items.explanation: Zero/First conditional: habitual action or real possibility.

items.sentence: It can get stressful at times, especially if visitors to the park (6) ___ (get) lost or injured.
items.explanation: Zero conditional: general truth/habit.

items.sentence: We had a case last week, which wouldn't (7) ___ (happen) if the woman involved (8) ___ (be) a bit more careful.
items.explanation: Third conditional result clause.

items.sentence: We had a case last week, which wouldn't have happened if the woman involved (8) ___ (be) a bit more careful.
items.explanation: Third conditional 'if' clause.

items.sentence: You see, if you (9) ___ (wander) around a large National Park like this without a map, you're bound to get lost.
items.explanation: First conditional: general consequence.

items.sentence: This particular case might (10) ___ (be) so bad if it (11) ___ (start) raining.
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past result with 'might'.

items.sentence: This particular case might not have been so bad if it (11) ___ (start) raining.
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past condition.

items.sentence: I dread to think what might (12) ___ (happen) if we hadn't!
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past result.

exercises.label: D
exercises.instruction: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: If we hadn't taken a wrong turning back there, we wouldn't ___ in this mess now.
items.explanation: Mixed conditional: Past condition (hadn't taken) -> Present result (now).

items.sentence: ___ you need any further assistance, please don't hesitate to ask.
items.explanation: Inverted first conditional: 'Should you need' = 'If you should need'.

items.sentence: ___ warned people in time, a lot more lives could have been lost.
items.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had they not' is correct. In inversion, 'not' comes after the subject.

items.sentence: If you ___ last weekend when the boss asked you, then you wouldn't have to work this weekend.
items.explanation: Mixed conditional: Past condition (had worked last weekend) -> Present result (wouldn't have to work now).

items.sentence: Were all the members of staff to attend the conference, would the hotel ___ enough rooms for everyone?
items.explanation: Inverted second conditional: 'Were... to attend' = 'If they attended'. Result is 'would have'.

items.sentence: Had the government acted sooner, the general public wouldn't ___ so much about the current situation.
items.explanation: Mixed conditional: Past condition (had acted) -> Present result (wouldn't be worrying now).

items.sentence: Tom Cruise wouldn't be the star he is today ___ made a good impression in his early films.
items.explanation: Mixed conditional: Present result (wouldn't be) -> Past condition (had he not made).

items.sentence: ___ build more roads, the traffic system would collapse by 2020.
items.explanation: Inverted second conditional. 'Not' comes after the subject.

items.sentence: It would be hard enough to pass the exam tomorrow even if you ___ to all the lectures this year.
items.explanation: Mixed conditional: Past condition (had gone to lectures earlier this year) -> Future/Present result (would be hard tomorrow).

items.sentence: You can contact the hotel manager on extension 142 ___ you need to.
items.explanation: Inverted first conditional (omitted 'if'): 'should you need to'.

items.sentence: ___ receive confirmation of your flight by e-mail, please click the link below.
items.explanation: Inverted first conditional negative form: 'Should + subject + not'.

items.sentence: I would have asked the person for ID before I let him in, if I ___ you.
items.explanation: Mixed conditional: Present condition (if I were you - constant state) -> Past result (would have asked).

exercises.label: E
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in the box.

items.sentence: Do you want a highly-paid career, and, if ___ , are you prepared to retrain? Contact us.
items.explanation: 'If so' replaces 'if you want a highly-paid career'.

items.sentence: I'm quite happy to lend you money, as ___ as you make sure I get it back.
items.explanation: 'As long as' means 'miễn là'.

items.sentence: The village fair should be a great success, ___ we don't have the same problems with the weather as last year.
items.explanation: 'Provided (that)' means 'với điều kiện là'.

items.sentence: It'd be great to see you on Saturday evening, ___ you've already got other plans.
items.explanation: 'Unless' means 'trừ khi' (if you don't have other plans).

items.sentence: Could you go to the shop for me? I have to stay in in ___ the courier comes.
items.explanation: 'In case' means 'phòng khi/trong trường hợp'.

items.sentence: Your idea sounds fine in theory, but just ___ something goes wrong. What then?
items.explanation: 'Suppose' is used to suggest a hypothetical situation.

items.sentence: Write your name at the top of your essay, ___ I won't know which one's yours.
items.explanation: 'Otherwise' means 'nếu không thì'.

items.sentence: The judge allowed the accused to stay at home on ___ she reported to the police every morning.
items.explanation: 'On condition (that)' means 'với điều kiện là'.

exercises.label: F
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I would never be able to afford a new house if ___ wasn't for all the overtime I've been doing.
items.explanation: Structure: 'If it wasn't/weren't for'.

items.sentence: If it hadn't ___ for the fact that his uncle was a partner in the business, Angus would never have got the job.
items.explanation: Structure: 'If it hadn't been for'.

items.sentence: How do you think you'd feel if someone happened ___ damage your car like that?
items.explanation: Structure: 'happen to do something'.

items.sentence: ___ for the kindness of a complete stranger, I would never have found the hotel.
items.explanation: 'But for' + noun means 'nếu không nhờ/nếu không vì'.

items.sentence: I don't think I'd be surprised in the slightest ___ Paul were to suddenly announce that he was moving abroad.
items.explanation: Standard second conditional with 'were to'.

items.sentence: Don't forget to mention the new schedule to Veronica if you ___ happen to see her this evening.
items.explanation: Structure: 'if you should happen to' (very unlikely/polite).

items.sentence: Football matches would be ideal family outings ___ it not for the danger of violence.
items.explanation: Inverted second conditional: 'Were it not for' = 'If it weren't for'.

items.sentence: If it hadn't been ___ the children, I'm sure Trisha would have left Tom a long time ago.
items.explanation: Structure: 'If it hadn't been for'.

items.sentence: I would have lost the game ___ it not been for some quick thinking.
items.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had it not been for'.

items.sentence: If you ___ find the book I was asking you about, give me a call.
items.explanation: 'If you should find' means 'nếu tình cờ tìm thấy'.

exercises.label: G
exercises.instruction: Rewrite the sentences, starting with the words given.

items.sentence: The only reason we won the contract was Debbie's fantastic presentation. -> We wouldn't have ___.
items.answer: won the contract if it hadn't been for Debbie's fantastic presentation
items.explanation: Third conditional using 'if it hadn't been for'.

items.sentence: The eventual arrival of the ferry prevented a fight from breaking out amongst the passengers. -> If it hadn't been for ___.
items.answer: the eventual arrival of the ferry, a fight would have broken out amongst the passengers
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past.

items.sentence: None of the passengers was injured because of the driver's quick reaction. -> But ___.
items.answer: for the driver's quick reaction, some of the passengers would have been injured
items.explanation: 'But for' + noun construction.

items.sentence: We'll have to cancel the meeting if Wendy doesn't turn up soon. -> Unless ___.
items.answer: Wendy turns up soon, we'll have to cancel the meeting
items.explanation: 'Unless' replaces 'if... not'.

items.sentence: Mr Jones would be quite lonely if he didn't have his daughter's visits. -> Were it ___.
items.answer: not for his daughter's visits, Mr Jones would be quite lonely
items.explanation: Inverted second conditional: 'Were it not for'.

items.sentence: Here's my phone number, because you might need it. -> In case ___.
items.answer: you need it, here's my phone number
items.explanation: 'In case' for precaution.

items.sentence: Agree to be back by midnight and you can go. -> Providing ___.
items.answer: you agree to be back by midnight, you can go
items.explanation: 'Providing' expresses a condition.

items.sentence: Officer Hughes prevented the robbers from escaping. -> Had it ___.
items.answer: not been for Officer Hughes, the robbers would have escaped
items.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had it not been for'.

items.sentence: If you saw another student cheating in an exam, ___ somebody?
items.explanation: Second conditional question (hypothetical present/future).

items.sentence: Make sure you mix the ingredients well, ___ you might get lumps in your cake.
items.explanation: 'Otherwise' means 'nếu không thì'.

items.sentence: Were ___ my dad, I would never have started playing tennis in the first place.
items.explanation: Inverted second conditional structure: 'Were it not for'.

items.sentence: If I ___ hear from Nigel, I'll tell him you were asking after him.
items.explanation: 'If I should happen to' is a polite/less likely first conditional form.

items.sentence: The book would have been perfect ___ the ending.
items.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had it not been for'.

items.sentence: If you hadn't taken the money, you ___ in prison now.
items.explanation: Mixed conditional: Past condition (hadn't taken) -> Present result (now).

items.sentence: Do you think that if we ___ earlier we might not have missed the coach?
items.explanation: Third conditional: hypothetical past.

items.sentence: Unless Ray ___ here soon, I'm going to leave because I'm getting bored.
items.explanation: 'Unless' is followed by an affirmative verb (Unless Ray gets = If Ray doesn't get).

items.sentence: What if I ___ you that there's a good chance I can get tickets for the concert?
items.explanation: 'What if' + second conditional 'were to tell' (hypothetical suggestion).

items.sentence: If there ___ happen to be any reason for late delivery, please inform our Accounts Department.
items.explanation: 'If there should happen to be' is a formal way to express a possibility.

exercises.label: I
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Had (1) ___ not been for the invention of CFCs in the 1920s, at least one environmental problem might have been avoided.
items.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had it not been for'.

items.sentence: In fact, (2) ___ they to be invented today, they (3) ___ no doubt be immediately banned.
items.explanation: Inverted second conditional: 'Were they to be'.

items.sentence: In fact, were they to be invented today, they (3) ___ no doubt be immediately banned.
items.explanation: Second conditional result clause.

items.sentence: For fifty years, though, if you bought an air conditioner or a spray, there (4) ___ a good chance that it contained CFCs.
items.explanation: Past real condition: 'If you bought... there was a chance'.

items.sentence: If you (5) ___ able to travel into the upper layers of the atmosphere, you (6) ___ see the chlorine and fluorine...
items.explanation: Second conditional: hypothetical present/future.

items.sentence: If you were able to travel into the upper layers of the atmosphere, you (6) ___ see the chlorine and fluorine...
items.explanation: Second conditional result clause.

items.sentence: Ozone could damage your lungs (7) ___ you were to breathe it...
items.explanation: Conditional linking word.

items.sentence: rays which can cause skin cancer if you (8) ___ exposed to them.
items.explanation: 'Are' for general fact (First conditional) or 'were' for hypothetical (Second).

items.sentence: We might (9) ___ have known about the process (10) ___ it not been for the work of two American scientists.
items.explanation: Negative adverb needed for the sense of the sentence.

items.sentence: We might never have known about the process (10) ___ it not been for the work of two American scientists.
items.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had it not been for'.

items.sentence: and might have won (11) ___ the evidence not been so clear.
items.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had the evidence not been'.

items.sentence: but (12) ___ which we might today be facing an even greater problem.
items.explanation: 'Without which' refers to the work of the scientists.

exercises.label: J
exercises.instruction: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence: Without this new diet, Henry would never have lost so much weight. (it) -> Had ___ this new diet, Henry would never have lost so much weight.
items.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had it not been for'.

items.sentence: Unless the plans change, we'll see you on the 19th. (no) -> Provided ___ the plans, we'll see you on the 19th.
items.explanation: Using 'provided' and 'no changes to' to mirror 'unless the plans change'.

items.sentence: If you applied now, there's a good chance you would get the job. (apply) -> Were ___ , there's a good chance you would get the job.
items.explanation: Inverted second conditional: 'Were you to apply'.

items.sentence: I didn't know about your problem so I didn't offer to help. (have) -> If I'd known about your problem, ___ to help.
items.explanation: Third conditional result clause.

items.sentence: It could get cold at night, so take some warm clothes. (in) -> Take some warm clothes ___ cold at night.
items.explanation: Precaution using 'in case'.

items.sentence: I'd love to travel the world if I didn't have to pay off my mortgage. (having) -> Were ___ to pay off my mortgage, I'd love to travel the world.
items.explanation: Inverted second conditional with 'having' as a gerund.

items.sentence: Ten years ago, people would have complained at seeing this film. (seen) -> Had ___ , they would have complained.
items.explanation: Inverted third conditional: 'Had they seen'.

items.sentence: Drivers are allowed to park here if they display a local resident permit. (condition) -> Drivers are allowed to park here ___ they display a local resident permit.
items.explanation: Using 'on condition (that)' as a conditional linker.
